channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Illinois man accused of deadly mass shooting at July 4th parade faces trial 3 years after attack
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill (AP) — The trial of a suburban Chicago man accused of a mass shooting at a 2022 Independence Day parade that killed seven people and wounded dozens more is set to begin Monday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan mother asks judge to declare 3 missing sons dead nearly 15 years later
item.description: ADRIAN, Mich. (AP) — A Michigan woman is asking a judge to declare her three missing sons dead, nearly 15 years after their father didn't return them after Thanksgiving. The disappearance has tormented a small town near Ohio and remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Foul! Cavaliers guard Ty Jerome blasts officials after whistle-happy crew calls 53 fouls
item.description: Upset with an inordinate number of personals called during Cleveland's 133-129 overtime win against Portland on Sunday, Jerome didn't hold back in criticizing the officiating crew — zeroing in on referee Natalie Sago.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at the Oscars
item.description: "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ariana Grande goes full Glinda, 'Wicked' bestie Erivo does Elphaba proud in green on Oscars carpet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ariana Grande did her Glinda proud in a pale pink stunner, and her “Wicked” bestie Cynthia Erivo honored Elphaba in deep forest green on the Oscars carpet as high glam took over Sunday. Others went for bold red, classic black and pops of pink.
item.dc:creator: LEANNE ITALIE and BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes today: Here’s what to look for at the show
item.description: Sunday’s Academy Awards will bring to a close one of the most unpredictable Oscar races in recent memory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: About 8 in 10 say they've recently experienced extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Overall, nearly 70 percent of adults believe in climate change, with 93 percent of Democrats and 51 percent of Republicans saying so, poll results show.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo launches political comeback with a run for New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo announced Saturday that he is running for mayor of New York City, relaunching his political career following a yearslong exile over a barrage of sexual harassment accusations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, WGN 9

item.title: Utah cheerleaders among evacuees after 'incident' at national competition in Texas
item.description: Utah cheerleaders and their family members were among the people evacuated from a Dallas, Texas, convention center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New charge filed against son of Buc-ee's co-founder
item.description: Mitchell Wasek, 29, is being charged with a third-degree felony offense of possession or promotion of child pornography.
item.dc:creator: Dalton Huey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect attempts suicide as slain brother laid to rest: report
item.description: A New Jersey man who is accused of sadistically killing his brother and trying to eat his eyeball attempted suicide inside Thursday, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cutoff of humanitarian parole for immigrants from Ukraine, 6 other countries challenged
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A group of American citizens and immigrants is suing the Trump administration for ending a long-standing legal tool presidents have used to allow people from countries where there's war or political instability to enter and temporarily live in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Flames from FedEx flight after bird strike near Newark Airport
item.description: A FedEx flight was forced to make an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport after a bird strike on Saturday morning, according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal weather and oceans agency touches people's daily lives in unnoticed ways, so massive firings there will likely cause needless deaths and a big hit to America's economy, according to the people who ran it.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Barbie celebrates the world of music with 2025 Career of the Year, Alicia Keys doll
item.description: Barbie is celebrating all things music this year, announcing its 2025 Career of the Year and a new doll, highlighting a popular musical artist.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Bryan Kohberger transcript, Princeton preppy murder, Gabby Petito's last days
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOD sends over 1,000 additional troops to help bolster southern border efforts
item.description: The Department of Defense is sending over 1,000 additional troops to the southern border to help bolster efforts to control illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania officer's last act after he was shot was to rescue a colleague, police chief says
item.description: A police officer who was shot to death while responding to an armed man with hostages inside a Pennsylvania hospital intensive care unit was remembered Friday as a dedicated public servant who did not hesitate to run toward danger and died a hero.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez family criticizes LA district attorney for opposing brothers' new trial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Cousins of Erik and Lyle Menendez spoke out Thursday criticizing the Los Angeles district attorney's recent decision to oppose a new trial for the brothers, who have spent nearly 30 years in prison for the 1989 killing of their parents.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No new bombshells in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn't include any new bombshells.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
